# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a React application. Install Node.js, run npm install, build the production bundle with npm run build, and serve it with a lightweight HTTP server. Keep the Dockerfile simple and easy to understand for our team.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as a parent image
FROM node:14-alpine

# Set the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /usr/src/app

# Copy package.json and npm install dependencies
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install

# Build React application for production
RUN npm run build

# Use Nginx to serve static files
FROM nginx:alpine

# Remove default index file
RUN rm /usr/share/nginx/html/*

# Copy built React app to Nginx's public directory
COPY --from=0 /usr/src/app/build /usr/share/nginx/html

# Expose port 80
EXPOSE 80

# Start Nginx server
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
